title: buxus natalensis
intro: Buxus natalensis, also known as the African boxwood or boxwood, is a small, evergreen shrub native to the eastern part of South Africa. It is a member of the Buxaceae family and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Buxus natalensis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, making it a valuable plant in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Buxus natalensis are often made by infusing the leaves in water or using them in tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
